recipes.title: Bobotie
recipes.description: Cape Malay baked dish of spiced minced meat topped with savory egg custard and bay leaves.

ingredients.name: white bread slices, soaked in milk

steps: Sauté the onions in oil until golden, about 8 minutes.
Add curry powder and turmeric, toast for 1 minute.
Add the ground beef and brown for 6 minutes, breaking it up.
Squeeze excess milk from the soaked bread, crumble bread into the meat.
Stir in apricot jam, raisins, vinegar, and salt. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Spoon mixture into a greased baking dish, smoothing the top.
Whisk eggs with the reserved milk and pour over the meat.
Tuck bay leaves on top and bake at 180°C for 35 minutes until set and golden.
Rest 5 minutes and serve with yellow rice.

recipes.title: Boerewors
recipes.description: South African farmer's coiled sausage of beef, pork, coriander, and clove, grilled over coals.
steps: Mix all spices, salt, and vinegar in a small bowl.
Combine beef, pork, and back fat in a chilled bowl.
Pour over the spice mix and knead briefly until just combined; do not overwork.
Cover and refrigerate for 4 hours to mature the flavors.
Rinse casings thoroughly and load onto a sausage stuffer.
Stuff into the casings, coiling into a single long spiral, twisting gently.
Prick lightly with a pin to release trapped air.
Grill over medium coals for 8-10 minutes per side until deep brown and just cooked through.
Rest for 3 minutes and serve with pap and chakalaka.

recipes.title: Bunny Chow
recipes.description: Durban Indian-South African dish of a hollowed bread loaf filled with spicy bean or mutton curry.

ingredients.name: Durban masala or hot curry powder

steps: Heat oil in a heavy pot and brown the lamb in batches for 6 minutes total.
Add the onion and cinnamon stick, cook 6 minutes.
Stir in garlic-ginger paste, masala, and turmeric. Toast 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes and salt; cook 8 minutes until pulpy.
Return meat to the pot with 500 ml water. Simmer covered 60 minutes.
Add potatoes and cook 25 more minutes until meat is tender and gravy is thick.
Cut the bread loaf into quarters and hollow out each quarter.
Spoon hot curry into the hollow, place the cut bread cube on top.
Garnish with coriander and serve with carrot sambals.

recipes.title: Chakalaka
recipes.description: South African spicy vegetable relish of onions, peppers, carrots, and beans simmered with curry.

ingredients.name: red and green bell peppers, diced

steps: Heat oil in a wide pan and sauté onion for 4 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and chili flakes. Cook for 1 minute.
Stir in curry powder and thyme; toast for 1 minute.
Add carrots and bell peppers, cook for 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and salt, simmer for 10 minutes until pulpy.
Stir in baked beans and warm through for 3 minutes.
Adjust salt and serve as a relish alongside pap, boerewors, or grilled meat.

recipes.title: Pap
recipes.description: South African stiff maize porridge, the everyday staple eaten with stews, relish, or boerewors.
steps: Bring water and salt to a rolling boil in a heavy pot.
Pour the maize meal in a slow stream into the boiling water without stirring.
Let it sit for 1 minute to form a mound, then poke a hole in the middle.
Cover and reduce heat to low; cook for 10 minutes.
Use a wooden spoon to vigorously mix the pap until smooth and thick.
Cover again and cook for another 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add a splash of water if too stiff, and stir until lump-free.
Serve hot in a mound alongside chakalaka, stew, or grilled meat.

recipes.title: Biltong
recipes.description: South African air-dried, spiced cured beef strips, leaner and tangier than jerky.
steps: Trim sinew from beef and slice along the grain into long 2cm-thick strips.
Mix salt, sugar, half the coriander, and pepper.
Layer the beef in a tray, dusting each layer with the cure.
Splash with brown vinegar and Worcestershire. Cover and refrigerate 4 hours.
Drain and pat the strips dry. Dust with the remaining coriander seeds.
Hook the strips and hang in a biltong dryer or breezy dry place at 18-22°C.
Dry for 4-7 days depending on desired moisture; should be firm but still slightly pliable.
Slice thinly across the grain and serve as a snack.

recipes.title: Potjiekos
recipes.description: Layered South African stew of meat, vegetables, and broth slow-cooked in a three-legged cast iron pot over coals.
steps: Heat oil in a cast iron pot over medium coals and brown the lamb for 8 minutes.
Remove the meat. Sauté onions and garlic for 4 minutes.
Return meat to the pot. Add wine and stock, season with salt, pepper, and bay leaves.
Layer carrots, then potatoes, then butternut, then green beans on top — do not stir.
Top with the chopped tomatoes.
Cover and let cook gently over low coals for 2.5 hours, never stirring.
Check liquid once and add a splash of stock if running dry.
When vegetables are tender, gently mix the layers together.
Serve from the pot with rice or pap.

recipes.title: Melktert
recipes.description: Cape Dutch milk tart with creamy cinnamon-dusted custard in a pastry shell.
steps: Roll the pastry and line a 24 cm tart tin. Blind-bake at 180°C for 15 minutes until golden.
Heat milk with the cinnamon stick and butter until just steaming. Discard cinnamon stick.
Whisk eggs with sugar, cake flour, cornflour, vanilla, and salt until smooth.
Pour a third of the warm milk into the egg mixture, whisking quickly.
Return the tempered mix to the saucepan and whisk over medium heat until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Pour the hot custard into the prebaked shell and smooth the top.
Dust generously with ground cinnamon.
Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate at least 2 hours before slicing.

recipes.title: Sosaties
recipes.description: Cape Malay marinated lamb skewers with apricots and onions, grilled over coals in curried sauce.
steps: Soak the dried apricots in warm water for 20 minutes, then drain.
Sauté chopped onion and garlic in oil for 4 minutes. Add curry powder and turmeric, toast 1 minute.
Add apricot jam, vinegar, salt, and bay leaves. Simmer 5 minutes; cool.
Combine lamb cubes with the marinade and refrigerate at least 8 hours.
Thread the lamb onto skewers alternating with onion wedges and apricots.
Reserve the marinade and warm it gently in a saucepan to use as a sauce.
Grill skewers over medium coals for 4 minutes per side, basting with marinade.
Rest 3 minutes and serve with yellow rice.

recipes.title: Koeksisters
recipes.description: South African plaited deep-fried dough drenched in cold spiced syrup, crisp on the outside and syrup-soaked within.
steps: Make the syrup: combine sugar, water, cinnamon, ginger, and lemon. Boil 5 minutes, then chill thoroughly in fridge.
Sift flour, baking powder, and salt. Rub in butter until crumbly.
Whisk milk and egg, add to flour and knead a smooth dough.
Rest dough covered for 1 hour.
Roll dough 8 mm thick. Cut into 8x2 cm rectangles, then slit each into three strips, plait, and pinch ends.
Heat oil to 175°C. Fry plaits for 3-4 minutes until golden.
Immediately drop hot koeksisters into the chilled syrup for 30 seconds, turning once.
Lift onto a rack to drain. Serve while crisp.

recipes.title: Malva Pudding
recipes.description: Cape Dutch warm sticky sponge pudding made with apricot jam and drenched in hot cream sauce.
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C and grease a square baking dish.
Whisk eggs and sugar until pale and thick.
Beat in jam, melted butter, milk, and vinegar.
Sift flour, baking soda, and salt over the wet mix and fold in.
Pour into the dish and bake 35-40 minutes until set and springy.
Make the sauce: heat cream, sugar, butter, hot water, and vanilla until just simmering.
Pierce holes in the hot pudding and slowly pour over the warm sauce.
Let it absorb 10 minutes, then serve warm with custard or ice cream.

recipes.title: Vetkoek
recipes.description: South African deep-fried bread rolls, crisp outside and pillowy inside, often filled with mince curry.
steps: Whisk flour, yeast, sugar, and salt in a large bowl.
Add warm water and stir into a soft, slightly sticky dough.
Knead 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover and let rise in a warm place for 60-75 minutes until doubled.
Punch down and divide into 12 portions; shape each into a round.
Rest the rounds covered for 15 minutes.
Heat oil to 165°C and fry vetkoek in batches for 4 minutes per side until deep golden and cooked through.
Drain on paper towels. Slit open and stuff with curried mince or jam.
